UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the EduTrack Attendance system, storing their profile details and role for access control. Authentication details (passwords) are managed by an external system (Firebase Auth).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firebaseAuthUid.description: The unique user ID provided by Firebase Authentication. This links the profile to the authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication (not for direct authentication data storage).

role.description: The role of the user, determining their access level within the application.

isActive.description: Indicates if the user's account is currently active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

AttendanceRecord.title: AttendanceRecord
AttendanceRecord.description: Records a specific attendance entry for a user, potentially including both clock-in and clock-out times, location data, and the method of marking. It can represent a daily work session or a manually overridden full-day attendance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AttendanceRecord entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who this attendance record belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AttendanceRecord)

date.description: The calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) for which this attendance record applies. Useful for date-based filtering.

entryDateTime.description: The timestamp when the user clocked in for this attendance session.

exitDateTime.description: The timestamp when the user clocked out for this attendance session. Null if the user is still clocked in or for a manual full-day override without explicit exit.

entryMethod.description: The method used to record the entry time (QR scan or manual entry by coordinator/admin).

exitMethod.description: The method used to record the exit time (QR scan or manual entry by coordinator/admin). Null if exit time is not recorded.

entryLocationLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate captured at the time of entry.

entryLocationLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate captured at the time of entry.

exitLocationLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate captured at the time of exit. Null if exit time is not recorded.

exitLocationLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate captured at the time of exit. Null if exit time is not recorded.

markedByCoordinatorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the coordinator or administrator who manually created or last modified this attendance record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AttendanceRecord)

isManualOverride.description: Indicates if this attendance record was created or significantly modified manually by a coordinator/administrator, overriding standard QR marking. This covers the 'Cumplió Jornada' scenario.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this attendance record was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this attendance record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, identified by their Firebase Auth UID. Includes the user's role for application-level access control. This collection serves as the primary source for user profile data, allowing direct owner access via `request.auth.uid` matching the document ID.

params.name: firebaseAuthUid
params.description: The unique user ID provided by Firebase Authentication, also serving as the document ID for the UserProfile.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for quickly checking if a user has the 'admin' role. The document ID is the user's Firebase Auth UID. Primarily used by security rules via `exists()` to grant elevated permissions, ensuring Authorization Independence without relying on `get()` calls to the main userProfiles collection.

params.name: firebaseAuthUid
params.description: The unique user ID provided by Firebase Authentication, also serving as the document ID for the admin role entry.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for quickly checking if a user has the 'coordinator' role. The document ID is the user's Firebase Auth UID. Primarily used by security rules via `exists()` to grant elevated permissions, ensuring Authorization Independence without relying on `get()` calls to the main userProfiles collection.

params.name: firebaseAuthUid
params.description: The unique user ID provided by Firebase Authentication, also serving as the document ID for the coordinator role entry.

definition.description: Stores individual attendance records. Each record belongs to a specific user, identified by `userId` (their Firebase Auth UID). This hierarchical structure enables simple Path-Based Ownership rules for teachers and efficient queries for a single user's history. Includes `userId` and `markedByCoordinatorId` (denormalized user ID) for Authorization Independence, allowing rules to verify ownership or administrative actions directly from document data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user who owns this attendance record.

params.name: attendanceRecordId
params.description: Unique identifier for the specific attendance record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Structural Segregation** to ensure robust and debuggable security rules. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Mandate A):**
1.  **User Roles:** Instead of relying on a `get()` call to a user's profile to determine their role (e.g., `get(/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role`), dedicated top-level collections are used for global roles: `/roles_admins/{firebaseAuthUid}` and `/roles_coordinators/{firebaseAuthUid}`. Security rules can then efficiently check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admins/$(request.auth.uid))` without introducing hierarchical authorization dependencies. This enables atomic operations and simplifies rule logic for administrators and coordinators. The `UserProfile` document itself still stores the `role` field as the source of truth for application UI, but security rules leverage the denormalized role collections.
2.  **Attendance Record Ownership:** Each `AttendanceRecord` document includes the `userId` field, which directly corresponds to the parent collection's `userId` wildcard (i.e., the owner's `firebaseAuthUid`). This allows teachers to access their own attendance records with a simple `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` check, again avoiding `get()` calls and ensuring authorization independence for user-owned data.
3.  **Manual Overrides:** The `markedByCoordinatorId` field within `AttendanceRecord` explicitly tracks who performed a manual override, aiding auditability and allowing rules to enforce that only authorized personnel can set this field.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs (Mandate B & C):**
1.  **User Profiles:** `/userProfiles/{firebaseAuthUid}` provides a dedicated space for user-specific profile data. Access is path-based (`request.auth.uid == firebaseAuthUid`) for the owner, with broader access granted to admins and coordinators via the denormalized role collections.
2.  **Attendance Records:** `/userProfiles/{userId}/attendanceRecords/{attendanceRecordId}` uses a hierarchical path structure, enforcing **Path-Based Ownership** (Mandate C). This ensures that a teacher can only list and manage their *own* attendance records securely (`request.auth.uid == userId`). For administrators and coordinators who need to access *all* records for reporting (RF-08, RF-09), the application design correctly leverages **Cloud Functions**, which have administrative privileges, to perform these aggregate queries. This completely avoids the anti-pattern of using security rules as filters (`QAPs`) on large client-side `list` operations, ensuring that client-side list queries within `/userProfiles/{userId}/attendanceRecords` return only authorized data (either the user's own, or for admins/coordinators, all for that specific `userId`).
3.  **Role Collections:** `/roles_admins/{firebaseAuthUid}` and `/roles_coordinators/{firebaseAuthUid}` are segregated collections, each with a homogeneous security posture (documents within these collections indicate a specific global role). This makes role-based access checks extremely clear and performant.

This structure directly supports the design principles by offering clear ownership, explicit role-based access, and eliminating complex `get()` calls in security rules, leading to a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable Firestore implementation.
